entry.title: Re: Defining BELNR and XBLNR intervals for a manually cre...
entry.content:
Hello!

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, bank statements created manually via Manage Bank Statements → Create do not use the BELNR/XBLNR interval restrictions defined in Electronic Bank Statements → Parameter Set Definition.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud has two different technical processing paths for bank statements:

  1. Electronic bank statements

Examples:

  • MT940 / CAMT via Multi‑Bank Connectivity
  • Upload Bank Statements
  • Manage Incoming Payment Files

For these parameter Set Definitions are applied

  1.  Manual bank statements

Examples:

  • Manage Bank Statements → Create
  • Uploading an excel spreadsheet into the Manage Bank Statements app

For these statements are treated as manual postings.

SAP Note 2979670 explicitly applies to Upload Bank Statements and Manage Incoming Payment Files. Does not cover:

  • Manually created bank statements
  • Excel uploads done via Manage Bank Statements → Create

In this scenario wrong debtor accounts get cleared even if interpretation algorithms and posting rules are working correctly because document reference restrictions are ignored and system:

  • finds a “best match” based on available references
  • may clear a different customer document with a similar number

 

The options to handle this cases will be:

Option 1:

If possible use Upload Bank Statements or Manage Incoming Payment Files even if the file is excel based.

This ensures that parameter set definitions are applied, BELNR / XBLNR intervals are respected, incorrect debtor clearing is prevented.

With this option you can keep same posting rules and same interpretation algorithms.

 

Option 2:

Disable automatic clearing for this bank account and reprocess items manually via Reprocess Bank Statement Items.

This does not fix the root cause, but it avoids incorrect postings.

- for the highlighted cells, the error message returned is "Invalid Date Format"

2) the same column "Date de début" has been generated by default as a Date dimension with hierarchical levels (Year, Quarter, month, Day) and it seems to be impossible to turn this column into a standard Generic dimension, 
as all other columns has been generated into. For the comparison step (with the 2nd file imported), I need to have all the columns as Generic dimensions, with the amount column as the only measure.

I send you some printscreens for help (attached file)

Could you please explain to me how to resolve these 2 errors ?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: CUSTOM CDS VIEW ON -VENDOR ACCOUNT STATEMENT
entry.content:
Hi @RAHULSOMANI ,

 

Here is the recommended approach for building a Vendor Account Statement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, based on your required table structure.

Foundation: 

In S/4HC Public Edition, direct table access (BSIK, BSAK, LFC1) is not available. All data must be sourced exclusively through released CDS Interface Views. The correct primary source for this use case is I_OperationalAcctgDocItem, this is important because it covers not just standard vendor line items but also other documents, which do not flow into ACDOCA. 

Fiori App: View Browser

 

CDS View field mapping for your table                                                

Your Column

Source CDS View

CDS Field Name

Posting Date

I_OperationalAcctgDocItem

PostingDate

Document Date

I_OperationalAcctgDocItem

DocumentDate

Narration / Item Text

I_OperationalAcctgDocItem

DocumentItemText

Document Type

I_OperationalAcctgDocItem

AccountingDocumentType

Document Type Nomenclature

I_AccountingDocumentType(joined)

AccountingDocumentTypeName

Debit

I_OperationalAcctgDocItem

AmountInCompanyCodeCurrency and DebitCreditCode WHERE DebitCreditCode = 'S'

Credit

I_OperationalAcctgDocItem

AmountInCompanyCodeCurrency and DebitCreditCode WHERE DebitCreditCode = 'H'

Closing Balance

Calculated field (running sum)

a running cumulative sum of Debit minus Credit, requiring a window function (this cannot be done in the Key User CDS tool and requires ABAP Cloud developer extensibility)

 

 

First, build the core data model using the Custom CDS Views app with I_OperationalAcctgDocItem as the primary source, joining I_AccountingDocumentType for the nomenclature column and I_WithholdingTaxItem for TDS amounts relevant to India.

Apply a base filter of AccountType = 'K' to scope the view to vendor lines only, with Supplier, CompanyCode, and PostingDate range as selection parameters.

Second, layer the Closing Balance running total on top using a custom CDS view developed in ABAP Cloud, which allows a SUM() OVER (PARTITION BY ... ORDER BY PostingDate) window function on top of the released interface view.

Once the CDS view is complete, expose it as a Custom Analytical Query via the Custom Analytical Queries app for consumption in SAC, a custom Fiori tile, or direct Excel export.

 

Relevant SAP Documentation

  • APIs for General Ledger Accounting

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b978f98fc5884ff2aeb10c8fdeb8a43b/610d1f7204d94644950928f575782f22.html

  • CDS Views for General Ledger Accounting

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/ca03bbfc892d4474be69934d6f5c783d.html

  • Explore SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, CDS Views on SAP Business Accelerator Hub

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/explore-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-cds-views-on-sap-business/ba-p/13519524

https://api.sap.com/package/SAPS4HANACloud/overview

 

Thank you,

Elena P.

entry.title: Re: BC Upgrade TR import issue
entry.content:
Hello!

From the message long text in attachment the key error is:

“Errors occurred during post‑handling /SMB/MD_AS_CUST_AIM for /SMB/MDCASUST T”

Important facts from the log:

  • The error occurs during post‑processing of a BC Upgrade / BC Scope Extension Transport Request
  • Object involved /SMB/MD_AS_CUST_AIM
  • Processing stops in AIM (Adaptation Implementation Model) post‑handling
  • Result: upgrade is interrupted and re‑import and manual import are blocked

This is a technical consistency failure.

/SMB/MD_AS_CUST_AIM is a reserved SAP internal object used for:

  • Business Configuration content
  • SSCUI-based scope extension
  • AIM transport orchestration
  • Synchronization between: SAP Best Practices content, CBC project scope, target system configuration

Based on SAP standard behavior for Public Cloud, this error typically occurs in one or more of the following situations:

  1. BC Upgrade TR and BC Scope Extension TR were mixed or reused.  

A transport generated for BC Upgrade was later reused / extended / reexported. Or a BC scope extension was attempted on top of a partially imported BC Upgrade

Result is AIM post‑processing detects inconsistent configuration state and import is forcibly stopped to protect the tenant

  1. Target system scope does not match CBC project anymore.  Examples:
  • Scope was changed again in CBC after TR generation
  • Another BC transport was already partially imported
  • QA/PRD system is no longer aligned with CBC source project
  1. Previous failed import left the system in a locked AIM state:
  • A failed BC import is not rollback safe
  • Reimporting the same TR is explicitly blocked by design
  • Manual import is also blocked for BC transports

 

The recommended (best approch) is to open a SAP incident.

In the incident can be mentioned: the original BC Upgrade TR ID, the BC Scope Extension TR ID, screenshots from your document, system (QA, PRD).

Then SAP depending on the root cause will analyze AIM consistency, reset internal BC state, repair the system, or invalidate the TR and instruct recreation from CBC.

To avoid further erros is not recommended to:

  • Retry imports repeatedly
  • Attempt manual import via alternative apps
  • Clone or copy the TR
  • Create additional scope changes before SAP intervention

entry.title: Never Be Surprised by Your AI Core Bill Again: Building a Consumption Monitor on SAP BTP
entry.content:
If you're running AI workloads on SAP AI Core, you've probably asked yourself at some point: "How much have we actually spent this month so far?" The SAP BTP Cockpit shows you the numbers — but only if you remember to check. And by the time you notice something is off, the month is already over.

This post walks through an open-source tool I built to solve exactly that: AI Core Consumption Monitor — a lightweight Python agent that runs on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry, checks your AI Core capacity unit usage daily, and sends you a notification before you hit your budget.

🔗 Open source on GitHub: github.com/SAP-samples/btp-ai-core-usage-monitor
🔗 AI4U project page: ai4u-website.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/project/btp-ai-core-usage-monitor

The Problem

Consider Dominik, a BTP Subaccount Administrator. His developers are running RAG pipelines, orchestration workflows, and chatbots — all consuming AI Core capacity units. He has no real-time visibility into how much has been spent. By the time the monthly report arrives, tens of thousands of euros may already be gone. The result: customer shock, escalations, and eroded trust in the platform.

SAP AI Core charges by capacity units (CU) — a normalized metric that aggregates token consumption across all models (Claude, GPT, embedding models, etc.). The usage data is available via the SAP BTP Usage Data Management API, but there's no built-in alerting when you approach a spending threshold.

What the Monitor Does

The agent runs as a background process on BTP Cloud Foundry and:

  1. Fetches month-to-date AI Core capacity unit usage from the UAS Reporting API (OAuth2, no manual token management)
  2. Aggregates consumption across all models and deployments
  3. Compares against your configured monthly spending limit
  4. Sends a notification based on the alert level:
    • ✅ Daily update — below warning threshold (configurable via RECEIVE_DAILY_UPDATE_EMAILS, default: on)
    • ⚠️ Warning — at 60% of limit (configurable via WARNING_THRESHOLD_PERCENT)
    • 🚨 Alert — at 80% of limit (configurable via ALERT_THRESHOLD_PERCENT)

The check runs automatically once a day at a configurable time (default: 07:00 UTC, set via CHECK_TIME_UTC). If you only want to be notified when thresholds are crossed — not every day — simply set RECEIVE_DAILY_UPDATE_EMAILS=false. You can also trigger a check on demand via a REST API call — useful for ad-hoc checks or integration into CI/CD pipelines.

What You Get in the Email

  • Colour-coded alert banner (green / amber / red)
  • Three key metrics: CU used, monthly limit, projected month-end
  • Visual progress bar (Outlook-compatible)
  • Per-model consumption breakdown table
  • Configured threshold reference

The projection is a simple linear extrapolation: if you've used X CU in the first N days of the month, you're on track to use X / N * days_in_month by month end. Not sophisticated, but surprisingly useful for catching runaway consumption early.

Two Notification Channels

Option A: SMTP Email

Works with any SMTP server — Office 365, Gmail, SendGrid, etc. The HTML email is built directly by the app, so you get the full rich layout regardless of your email client.

Option B: SAP Alert Notification Service (ANS)

When ENABLE_ANS=true, the agent posts a structured event to the ANS Producer API. ANS then routes it to whatever channel you've configured — email, Slack, Microsoft Teams, PagerDuty, or any other action in your ANS instance.

The event carries structured tags (subaccountId, percentageUsed, projectedCu, periodStart, periodEnd, colour tokens) that you can use in ANS Payload Templates to build a custom HTML email directly in ANS — without any SMTP server.

Architecture

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  SAP BTP Cloud Foundry                                      │
│                                                             │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │  ai-core-monitor (Python / Flask)                    │   │
│  │                                                      │   │
│  │  APScheduler ──► run_monitoring_job() [daily 07:00]  │   │
│  │       │                                              │   │
│  │       ▼                                              │   │
│  │  UAS Reporting API ──► fetch usage (OAuth2)          │   │
│  │       │                                              │   │
│  │       ▼                                              │   │
│  │  Aggregate ai-core capacity_units                    │   │
│  │       │                                              │   │
│  │       ▼                                              │   │
│  │  Compare vs. SPENDING_LIMIT                          │   │
│  │       │                                              │   │
│  │       ├── < 60%  → INFO  (daily email if enabled)    │   │
│  │       ├── ≥ 60%  → WARNING email                     │   │
│  │       └── ≥ 80%  → ALERT email                       │   │
│  │                                                      │   │
│  │  Flask endpoints: /health  /status  /trigger         │   │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

The app is a standard Python Flask application — 256 MB memory, one CF instance, no database required. It uses:

  • APScheduler for the daily cron job
  • Requests for the UAS and ANS API calls
  • Gunicorn as the WSGI server
  • XSUAA for JWT-based protection of the REST endpoints

Getting Started

The full deployment takes about 10 minutes:

# 1. Clone the repo
git clone https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-ai-core-usage-monitor.git
cd btp-ai-core-usage-monitor

# 2. Create required CF services
cf create-service xsuaa application ai-core-monitor-xsuaa -c xs-security.json
cf create-service uas reporting-ga-admin uas-reporting

# 3. Edit manifest.yml — set your subaccount ID, spending limit, SMTP details
# 4. Deploy
cf push

# 5. Set your SMTP password (never in manifest.yml)
cf set-env ai-core-monitor SMTP_PASSWORD 'your-smtp-password'
cf restart ai-core-monitor

# 6. Verify
curl https://<your-app-url>/health

The minimum configuration is three environment variables:

env:
  SUBACCOUNT_ID: "your-subaccount-uuid"
  SPENDING_LIMIT: "100"
  NOTIFICATION_EMAIL: "your-team@company.com"

The full set of configurable parameters:

Parameter Default Description

SUBACCOUNT_ID—SAP BTP subaccount UUID to monitor
SPENDING_LIMIT100Monthly AI Core capacity unit budget
WARNING_THRESHOLD_PERCENT60% of limit that triggers a warning notification
ALERT_THRESHOLD_PERCENT80% of limit that triggers an alert notification
RECEIVE_DAILY_UPDATE_EMAILStrueSend a daily summary even when below the warning threshold
CHECK_TIME_UTC07:00Time of day (UTC) to run the daily check
NOTIFICATION_EMAIL—Comma-separated recipient email addresses
SENDER_NAMEAI Core Consumption MonitorDisplay name in the From field
ENABLE_SMTPtrueEnable SMTP email notifications
SMTP_HOST—SMTP server hostname (e.g. smtp.office365.com)
SMTP_PORT587SMTP port
SMTP_USER—SMTP login username
SMTP_PASSWORD—SMTP password (set via cf set-env, never in manifest.yml)
SMTP_USE_TLStrueUse STARTTLS (true) or SMTP_SSL (false)
ENABLE_ANSfalseEnable SAP Alert Notification Service instead of / in addition to SMTP
ANS_SERVICE_NAMEai-core-monitor-ansCF service instance name for ANS (auto-detected if only one ANS instance is bound)

REST API

The app exposes a small REST API for operational use:

Endpoint Description

GET /healthLiveness probe (no auth)
GET /statusLast monitoring result as JSON
GET /configActive configuration (credentials masked)
POST /triggerRun a check immediately

The /trigger endpoint accepts {"dry_run": true} to fetch and compute usage without sending any notifications — useful for testing your setup.

Try It

The project is open source under Apache 2.0:

👉 github.com/SAP-samples/btp-ai-core-usage-monitor

👉 AI4U project page: ai4u-website.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/project/btp-ai-core-usage-monitor

If you find it useful, have questions, or want to contribute — issues and pull requests are welcome.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA configuration for SAC pull of plan data
entry.content:
@TarkanCitirgi 


Dear Tarkan,
Thank you very much for the detailed and clarifying response — this is very helpful and aligns well with our understanding.
We confirm that your interpretation of our setup (A), (B), and (C) is correct. Based on your guidance, we plan to proceed with the following approach:

1. Revenue Planning – Multiple SAC Models (A)

Each Line of Business performs revenue planning in a dedicated SAC model.
All revenue planning models write back to a shared revenue planning category (e.g. PLN_REV01 / FCST_M01–M12).
Data separation is ensured through:

Use of an S/4 organizational field representing the Line of Business (e.g. Business Area / Profit Center).
Explicit inclusion of this field in the Plan Data Scope.


As the Revenue GL accounts and organizational dimensions are part of the scope, overwrite risks are avoided.


2. OPEX Planning – Central Model (B)

All Lines of Business perform cost planning in a single central OPEX SAC model.
OPEX data is written to a separate planning category (e.g. PLN_OPEX1 / FCST_M01–M12).
This avoids overlap with revenue planning, as different GL accounts are used and GL Account is included in the Plan Data Scope.
We acknowledge the importance of ensuring that deleted or changed GL accounts in SAC are consistently reflected in S/4HANA.


3. CAPEX Planning

CAPEX planning is handled in a dedicated SAC CAPEX model across all Lines of Business.
CAPEX data is written to budget‑specific planning categories (e.g. BUD_CAPEX / FCSTCEX_M01–M12).
This aligns with SAP’s recommendation, as budget‑specific planning categories are required to trigger budget‑related functionality in S/4HANA (e.g. availability control and budget reporting).
CAPEX planning categories are kept fully isolated from Revenue and OPEX categories to prevent any overlap.


4. Consolidated / Financial Statement Planning (C)

We fully agree that the consolidated financial statement model must not write back into the same planning category used for Revenue or OPEX.
As this model typically plans using the same GL accounts, reusing the same category could lead to:

Data overwrites
Duplicate figures in S/4 reporting


Therefore, the consolidated model writes to a dedicated planning category (e.g. BUDG_FS01), used purely for group‑level financial reporting.


5. Guiding Principle Applied

A shared planning category is used only where the Plan Data Scope is fully disjoint (via GL accounts and/or organizational dimensions).
Where overlap is possible (especially consolidation and budget scenarios), data is separated by planning category, not by configuration ID alone, in line with the overwrite behavior you described.

Finally, Could you please confirm whether SAP recommends using SAP‑delivered planning categories by default, and creating custom planning categories only when functional or reporting separation is required, rather than as a general rule for each SAC model?  

thank you for clarifying the role of the SAP‑delivered PLN category and the rationale behind the budget‑specific import templates — this aligns well with our CAPEX and budget planning approach.

Hello @Nicolas_BacaStorni 

My few cents on this topic with an assumption that you are keeping the term ERP more generic:
ERP systems will provide the essential data foundation and business process structure that AI requires to deliver meaningful insights.
New age solutions like SAP S/4HANA has integrated AI (Business AI) capabilities directly embedded into core business processes, enabling automated exception handling, predictive analytics, and intelligent automation across different Lob's like finance, supply chain, and operations.
Although in the Legacy ECC world, things might not be so sweet compared to new solutions like S/4.

BR, Prabhith

entry.title: Re: From Numbers to Narrative: Building an AI Analytics Assistant in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
@spurwar 

Just a thought, Over last month I have seen a lot of blogs and experiments over how AI assisted custom widget development can enhance the SAC experience. For me the larger question is how resilient are these custom widgets developed with AI assisted coding to QRC upgrades. The moment these widgets go in production, it becomes a technical debt if a QRC upgrade breaks it as SAP would not support it and AI vibe coding may or may not be able to fix it.

Also the second thing this flurry of custom widgets will raise is if its that simple to enhance the experience with AI vibe coded widgets, SAP should roll it out as a standard features.

For e.g. You could vibe code a date picker for your stories for Date selections and AI would create that widget in few minutes but what happens once you roll that out and it breaks after a QRC upgrade.

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud J62: Asset Quantity Resulting fr...
entry.content:
Hello!

 

In direct capitalization (J62) SAP derives asset quantity from procurement quantities. Each capitalization line contributes to value and quantity.

SAP does not distinguish between “component quantity” and “physical asset count”

This ensures FI consistency and follows a line-item–driven accounting model, not a physical-asset model.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is not possible to:

  • override the system derived asset quantity
  • decouple asset quantity from procurement quantity
  • add custom logic in J62 to force quantity = 1

 

While the behavior cannot be changed, the following approaches are commonly used:

Option 1:

Treat the asset quantity as “Component Count”. Asset quantity = number of capitalized components and use description / notes / classification to reflect 1 Physical Asset.

Option 2:

Use asset main number + subnumbers. Create one main asset (physical asset) plus multiple sub‑assets for components. The result will be

  • Main asset = quantity 1
  • Sub assets represent components

Option 4:

Reporting‑level correction.

Adjust asset inventory reports by showing physical asset count = 1 and treat quantity as informational.

entry.title: Re: Errors when trying to import a csv file into a model
entry.content:
@Alex_Unige_2024 

The reason you see errors is because your date format in file and date format in wrangler cannot be different. Initially SAC checks only 2000 rows of data for validation. You cannot have a mix format in the same date column so select the date format as per the date column in file which is MMDDYYYY.

 

You can also change the type of field on the right pane in the wrangler. So from Date you can change to string.

 

 

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Intercompany Projects AR Invoice - Itemization required
entry.content:
The Standard behavior is intercompany AR invoice consolidates amounts at the role level, even if the same role is used across multiple projects. That is why you see one line item per role, not per project.

Traceability options:

  • Use the Project Billing Data Source CDS views (e.g., I_ProjectBillingItem, I_ProjectBillingDocument) to report time and amounts at project + role level.
  • Combine with Resource-Related Billing CDS views to drill down to the original time postings.
  • This gives you the granularity in reporting, even though the invoice itself remains consolidated.

Build a query on these CDS views to expose project, role, hours, and amounts. This is the recommended way to achieve end-to-end traceability without changing invoice line item logic.

Having said that the practical approach can be

  • Invoices will remain consolidated at role level; SAP does not split by project in the AR invoice. Use CDS views for detailed reporting.
  • External contractor costs flow through the provider project; you can trace them via CDS joins, but not directly link the supplier invoice to the customer project.
  • Best practice: Build a Custom Analytical Query that combines:
    • Project billing items (granularity by project + role)
    • Purchase order and supplier invoice items (for contractor costs)
    • Intercompany billing documents (for the transfer)

This way you achieve the end-to-end traceability you’re looking for, even though the invoice itself is summarized.

entry.title: Re: SSAM Switch Persona
entry.content:
Due to upload size restrictions to the play store the demo db for android had to be scaled back so it doesn't have all the same data as iOS currently.  The SSAM team is working on an alternate solution for the demo db but I don't know a timeline.

If you are unable to switch personas in a live environment then you will need to get the additional personas assigned to your user in the backend admin configuration.  Keep in mind there may be licensing impacts to doing this.

entry.title: Re: From Numbers to Narrative: Building an AI Analytics Assistant in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @N1kh1l ,

 

That’s fair, and I completely agree that custom widgets themselves are not new — AI just changes the speed at which they can now be created.

In this case, the widget still follows existing SAC widget guidelines and, like any other custom extension, ownership and maintenance ultimately sit with the customer/end user deploying it.

I also intentionally tried to avoid pushing heavy business logic into the widget layer itself. Most calculations, aggregations, and transformations are handled through SAC models/data bindings so the widget remains primarily an orchestration and presentation layer rather than a parallel calculation engine.

On the visualization side, SAC already natively supports the charts and components used here. If you break the experience down tab-by-tab, much of the functionality already exists inside SAC today in some form.

The experiment was less about replacing SAC native capabilities and more about exploring whether those capabilities could be combined into a more unified, AI-assisted workflow experience within a single interface.

And yes — based on the current roadmap, several areas mentioned in the blog, especially AI-generated insights and assisted analysis experiences, are clearly moving toward becoming native SAC capabilities over time.

Regards

Shivam

entry.title: Four years of recognition: SAP Signavio again a Leader in the Gartner® Magic Quadrant™
entry.content:
This year again, SAP Signavio has been recognized as a Leader in the Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ — this time in an expanded and newly defined category: Process Intelligence Platforms.

Process intelligence platforms unify process mining, modeling, and monitoring to help enterprises visualize, analyze, and automate processes. As AI scales, organizations can leverage these tools to provide the operational context needed to plan and prioritize the best areas to deploy AI agents.

Download here a complimentary copy of the report

We believe this broader lens fully captures the scope of what process intelligence can deliver for organizations today, driving greater enterprise observability, smarter decision-making, accelerated transformation, and measurable business outcomes. In SAP Signavio, this is made possible through one unified suite, empowered by deep AI capabilities, intelligent agents and assistants.

A wider lens on Process Intelligence

The Gartner Magic Quadrant provides a clear, research-based overview of the evaluated vendors in a given market. This year’s Magic Quadrant assessed 13 vendors across a range of criteria, including market understanding, offering strategy, innovation, product and service quality, customer experience, and more.

In the report Gartner notes: “With the rapid rise of agentic AI and the subsequent need for process automation, traditional, isolated process analysis tools are failing to meet evolving demands. Process intelligence has matured to offer unified visibility and actionable insights, shifting organizations from reactive troubleshooting to proactive, continuous improvement”

Why we believe we are a Leader

"We have been front runners in process intelligence for the last decade. We hold firm to the idea that modeling, mining, and transformation management belong in a single, unified experience — and we have built our suite around exactly that conviction," says Alessandro Manzi, Head of Product Management at SAP Signavio.”We believe, the acknowledgment of SAP Signavio as a Leader of this unified offering reflects our dedication to helping organizations harness AI with complete trust and governance, but also tied  to business outcomes. Being named a Leader for the fourth consecutive year motivates us to keep innovating our space and building what truly matters for our customers”       

With SAP Signavio solutions you can:

  • Achieve fuller enterprise observability and surface actionable insights with an end-to-end integrated suite.
  • Identify, prioritize, and prove the value of your transformation initiatives, track and showcase ROI, and maximize business impact.
  • Reach full potential with embedded AI capabilities and agents that make everyday tasks easier and accelerate decision-making.                     .

Download here a complimentary copy of this year’s Gartner Magic Quadrant for Process Intelligence from SAP Signavio. 

Gartner, Magic Quadrant for Process Intelligence Platforms, Tushar Srivastava, David Sugden, Marc Kerremans, 5 May 2026.

Gartner and Magic Quadrant are trademarks of Gartner, Inc. and/or its affiliates. Gartner does not endorse any company, vendor, product or service depicted in its publications, and does not advise technology users to select only those vendors with the highest ratings or other designation. Gartner publications consist of the opinions of Gartner’s business and technology insights organization and should not be construed as statements of fact. Gartner disclaims all warranties, expressed or implied, with respect to this publication, including any warranties of merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose.

Gartner and Magic Quadrant are trademarks Gartner, Inc., and/or its affiliates.

entry.title: want to undo migration from global hierarchies back to financial statement version
entry.content:
How to undo migration for financial statement version to manage global hierarchies.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: want to undo migration from global hierarchies back t...
entry.content:
Undo the financial statement version migration to manage global hierarchies from the configuration activity Migrate Financial Statement Versions to Global Hierarchies.

  1. Open configuration activity Migrate Financial Statement Versions to Global Hierarchies.
  2. Open details of hierarchy type FSVN.
  3. Select dropdown to filter migrated hierarchies.
  4. Select the migrated hierarchy you want to undo.
  5. Press Undo Migration button.
  6. Confirm Undo Migration.

entry.title: You Asked for Simpler. SAP Signavio Listened. Here Is What Changes.
entry.content:
On May 26, 2026, SAP Signavio is releasing two interconnected updates that will change how you navigate and work within the suite. Together, they represent the most significant user-experience improvement to SAP Signavio in recent years.

The first update is a fully redesigned, unified navigation: one consistent side navigation connecting all SAP Signavio capabilities, a new personalized homepage called My Page, and SAP WalkMe integrated directly in the header to guide you through the changes.

The second update unifies SAP Signavio Process Insights and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence into a single product and interface under the name SAP Signavio Process Intelligence.

This guide covers both changes in detail, so you know exactly what to expect, what has moved, and what this means for your day-to-day work.

The New SAP Signavio Suite Navigation

The goal of this change is simple: one home for everything, structured around how you work, instead of navigating multiple disconnected menus or relying on bookmarks to find capabilities.

Everything in SAP Signavio is now accessible from a single, unified place.

1. The App Switcher Is Gone

The app switcher (grid icon in the top bar) has been removed.

Every SAP Signavio capability is now accessible directly from the unified left-side navigation.

2. New Navigation Structure

The left-side navigation is organized into consistent areas:

  • Quick Access (top): Your personal starting point: Home (My Page), Process Consulting Agent, Newsfeed, Favorites, Recents, and Tasks.
  • Transformation Journey (middle): Capabilities grouped by phase: Browse and Manage | Transform and Realize Value | Mine and Analyze (it will be explained in detail below) | Model and Govern.
  • Administration (bottom): Lab Space, Reporting, User Management, and Workspace Settings

Whatever you are working on, the structure is always the same. You always know where you are and what comes next along the transformation journey.

3. My Page: Your New Personalized Home

My Page is a new personalized homepage within SAP Signavio. It is your own space to quickly access what you care about, what you have been working on, and where you need to go next.

Three widgets are available at launch:

  • Favorites: items you have starred.
  • Recents: recently visited process models and capabilities.
  • Quick Links: user-defined links to any URL (inside or outside Signavio), pinned directly on your homepage.

You can drag and drop widgets to rearrange your layout and add additional widgets via the Add widget button in the top right corner.

More widgets from other product areas are planned for future releases.

 
Note: My Page is available to advanced users (anyone with more than a basic Hub license). It is intentionally not available to process participants who log in infrequently. Admins can disable My Page via a feature flag. There are three home experiences in total: the original Landing Page, My Page (new), and My Process Overview (released November 2025).
 

 

4. Header Changes: Workspace Switch and Preview/Publish Mode

Two controls that used to be buried in the user profile menu are now always visible in the header:

  • Workspace Switcher: promoted from the user menu to the header, right next to the Signavio logo. The active workspace is always visible, making multi-workspace navigation faster and more intuitive.
  • Preview/Publish Mode Indicator: moved from the user menu to the header. When in preview mode, the header background turns yellow as a clear visual signal. The dashed banner that previously appeared in the content area is gone.

5. User Menu: What Changed

Several items in the user menu have been updated or reorganized:

  1. Personal Settings renamed to Settings.
  2. Audiences and content language settings remain unchanged.
  3. Content Language remain unchanged.
  4. Legal Information (privacy policy, terms of use) added and now accessible from one place across all capabilities.
  5. Workspace ID and Product Version moved to a new About dialog.
  6. Manage Account added with an explicit label (navigates to the user profile, same behavior as before).

Note: Admins can still configure the header background color and upload a custom logo in workspace settings.

6. Terminology Changes to Know

Several names have been updated.

Functionality has not changed, only the names:

PREVIOUS TERM

NEW TERM

Shared Documents

Repository

Root Folder (inside Repository)

Modeling Files

SAP Signavio Process Governance (in App Switcher)

Governance Workflows

Process (in SAP Signavio Process Governance)

Workflow

Activity (in SAP Signavio Process Governance)

Action

Analytics (in SAP Signavio Process Governance)

Reporting

Personal Settings (in User Menu)

Settings

7. SAP WalkMe: In-App Guided Tours from Day One

SAP WalkMe is now integrated directly into the SAP Signavio header and is available across all capabilities. It provides guided in-app walkthroughs to help users orient to the new navigation from day one.

On first login after the May 26 release, you will see a WalkMe welcome screen. Clicking "Show me around" launches a guided tour covering the new side navigation, user menu, workspace switcher, and the WalkMe panel itself.

 

At launch, four guided tours go live:
  • Two covering the new suite navigation (side navigation and Process Manager navigation).
  • Two covering SAP Signavio Process Intelligence topics (including Create Dashboard).

Additional guided flows will be introduced to help users discover and make the most of our features.

All embedded tours are provided by SAP Signavio at no extra cost, available to all users across all license types, and launch in English (translations planned for the second half of 2026).

Note: A premium WalkMe option is available as a paid add-on for organizations that want to build their own custom guided flows tailored to their specific processes. Admins manage WalkMe settings in Workspace Settings under WalkMe Digital Adoption Tool.

8. Your workspace, simplified

  • Fewer Tabs, less noise: The new navigation, cuts down the number of open browser tabs, so your workflow stays clean and your focus stays sharp.
  • Built for Your Role: Each user sees only the capabilities relevant to their role, license, and access settings. Whether you are a process analyst, a modeler, or an administrator, your navigation looks exactly like it should.

 9. Admins Only: Explore Three Flexible Display Modes

Administrators can set the default state of the side navigation in workspace settings, or configure it per audience:

  • Expanded: fully visible with labels, ideal for orientation and onboarding.
  • Collapsed (Icons Only): shows icons only for a focused, compact view. Used by default in areas like the repository to preserve content space.
  • Off-Screen (Overlay): the navigation slides in on demand via the hamburger menu. Used in canvas-based tools like Process Modeler where screen real estate matters. The full navigation is always one click away.

Note: Users can manually expand or collapse the navigation during their session by clicking the hamburger menu. The admin-configured default is restored on the next login.

 

 
10. Special information for Administrators
As an administrator, please complete the following steps after the Signavio release :

 

  • Review and configure the default navigation state for your organization: expanded, icons only, or completely off screen (overlay mode) in the navigation section within your workspace settings.
  • Review audience and access configurations to ensure each user group sees the navigation item relevant to their role.
  • Communicate this change to your end users, so they are prepared for the updated experience when they log in.
  • If your organization holds a WalkMe Premium license, you must enter your WalkMe System URL to activate your customized digital adoption experience. To do this, navigate to Workspace Settings – Help resources – WalkMe Digital adoption platform, select “and paste your System URL in the field provided.

New Navigation in Process Modeler

Process Modeler uses a canvas-based workspace where screen space is critical. The new navigation handles this through the overlay (off-screen) mode.

  • When you open Process Modeler, the left-side navigation automatically hides to give the canvas its full workspace.
  • To access the navigation, click the hamburger menu (≡) at any time. The side navigation slides in as an overlay without navigating away from your current work.
  • All capabilities previously accessible via the user menu inside Process Modeler (such as Dictionary, Explorer, Quick Model, and Graphical Editor) are now listed under the Process Manager entry in the side navigation.

Note: Governance Workflow navigation is partially integrated in this release. When navigating to Governance Workflows, the overlay navigation does not yet display the full list of capabilities. Users can click "Back to Home" to return to the Signavio workspace. Full integration is planned.

What to Expect on May 26, 2026

  • The new navigation, My Page, and WalkMe go live simultaneously for all SAP Signavio users on May 26, 2026. No action is required from end users to access the new navigation. It is available from the first login after the release date.
  • WalkMe guided tours will greet you on your first login with a "Show me around" prompt. These tours are free, available in English, and walk you through every major change. You can return to them at any time via the WalkMe icon in the header.
  • The SAP Help Portal documentation is being updated in parallel. An additional entry point reflecting the unified Signavio experience will be available from May 26. Existing documentation by product and SKU will co-exist during the transition period.

entry.title: New Course Launch: Streamlining Customer Support and Orders with SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
We are happy to announce the launch of our new course in learning.sap.com platform:

Streamlining Customer Support and Orders with SAP Commerce Cloud 

This course is designed to help learners understand how SAP Commerce Cloud supports end‑to‑end customer service and order handling—efficiently, consistently, and at scale.

⭐Why This Course Matters

Whether you are working in customer support, sales operations, consulting, or solution architecture, understanding how customer interactions and orders are handled in SAP Commerce Cloud is critical for various reasons:

Customer Experience Is Everyone’s Business

Support agents and sales reps are often the first point of contact for customers. Slow resolution times, inconsistent order handling, or missing information can quickly impact trust. This course shows how SAP Commerce Cloud enables:

  • Faster issue resolution
  • Accurate order handling
  • A consistent customer experience across touchpoints

Reduce Complexity, Increase Confidence

SAP Commerce Cloud offers powerful capabilities—but knowing how and when to use them makes all the difference. This course helps learners understand:

  • Key support and order management concepts
  • How different roles interact with the platform
  • How standard processes reduce errors and rework

🎯 Learning Objectives

This course is structured around practical, job‑relevant units that guide learners through the essentials of customer support and order handling in SAP Commerce Cloud. By completing each unit, learners will gain the following skills:

  • Introducing Order Management in SAP Commerce Cloud: This unit introduces the fundamentals of order management in SAP Commerce Cloud and explains its role in delivering seamless, end‑to‑end customer experiences.
  • Setting Up Your Order Management Configuration: Learners explore how order management is configured in SAP Commerce Cloud and understand how the right setup enables efficient and accurate order handling.
  • Processing Orders: This unit walks through the complete order lifecycle, helping learners understand how orders are processed, tracked, and managed throughout their various stages.
  • Supporting Customers: Learners discover how SAP Commerce Cloud supports customer service activities, enabling support teams to handle order inquiries, issues, and updates effectively.
  • Managing Product Returns, Reviews, and Protecting Customer Data: This unit covers post‑purchase processes such as managing returns and customer reviews, while also emphasizing best practices for protecting customer data and ensuring privacy.

✅ Who Should Attend?

The course aims to:

  • Business Users
  • SAP Commerce Cloud Consultants
  • Business analysts

🏅Earn a Knowledge Badge

Course completion rewards learners with a digital knowledge badge, demonstrating their skills in SAP Commerce Cloud customer support and order management—perfect for sharing achievements both internally and on professional networks.

📢Spread the Word!

Help us share this exciting update with partners, colleagues, and other stakeholders.

📝We Value Your Feedback

After completing the certification, please take a moment to share your feedback. Your insights help us continuously improve the learning experience.
 
Two easy ways to share feedback!
  1. Click “Share Feedback” to submit your thoughts. 

     

     

     

  2. In the “Notification Center” under “Learning” you can select the congratulatory email after completing course or learning journey and click “Feedback” to share your thoughts.

entry.title: SAP BTP - API Management with SDIA — GDCR & DDCR in Practice
entry.content:
"The proxy never changes. The KVM grows."

Introduction

This is not a product. There is no license fee, no consulting package, and no vendor behind it.

SDIA — Semantic Domain Integration Architecture — was created in three months, solo, without funding, driven by one frustration I kept seeing across SAP BTP projects: the same mistakes repeated at scale.

Dozens of proxies doing the same thing.
No deterministic naming convention.
Credentials mixed across domains.
A developer required for every new route.
A deployment cycle for every new endpoint.
Monitoring showing dozens of iFlows doing variations of the same architectural function.

I built SDIA because the SAP community deserved better than that.

SDIA lives in the Domain Layer. It is governance, not code. It is architecture, not a framework you install. Anyone can adopt it, adapt it, and evolve it — that is the point.

This blog demonstrates two SDIA patterns applied to SAP BTP API Management:

Pattern Role
GDCR — Gateway Domain-Centric RoutingImmutable semantic gateway facade
DDCR — Domain Driven-Centric RouterDeterministic runtime resolution engine

Both are running live in the demo environment shown in the video. Every proxy, every KVM entry, and every Newman call used in the demonstration is available on GitHub for download and testing.

 

The Problem This Solves

When teams migrate to SAP BTP Integration Suite, a predictable collapse often happens. What starts as a clean slate becomes:

  • one proxy per interface, vendor, or backend system;
  • no consistent naming across packages;
  • credentials shared across domains;
  • every new API route requiring code changes and redeployment;
  • monitoring dashboards filled with many artifacts doing variations of the same job.

This is what SDIA calls Endpoint Chaos and Package Sprawl.

It is not incompetence. It is the natural result of building without a governing principle.

The governing principle SDIA introduces is simple:

The domain is the primary key of every integration artifact.

From the API gateway to the integration layer to the data tier — one semantic key, applied consistently, reduces structural chaos.

This is what happens when enterprise integration is indexed by tools, vendors, projects, and protocols instead of domains.

This is not a tooling problem. It is an indexing problem.

The landscape is indexed by technology. SDIA re-indexes it by domain.

What SDIA Is

SDIA — Semantic Domain Integration Architecture — is a vendor-agnostic governance model. It defines how integration artifacts are named, how packages are structured, how credentials are segmented, and how routing decisions are made across the enterprise integration landscape.

For this blog, three layers matter:

Layer Pattern Governs
Layer 1GDCRSAP BTP API Management — semantic gateway facade
Layer 2DDCRSAP BTP API Management — deterministic runtime resolution
Layer 3ODCPSAP Cloud Integration — iFlow, package, credential, and Value Mapping governance

GDCR and DDCR operate inside SAP API Management. ODCP operates inside SAP Cloud Integration.

Together, they form an immutable routing pipeline where the deployed artifact does not change when a new route is added. The metadata grows.

The same principle was also applied to SAP Cloud Integration using ODCP and Value Mapping:

Companion implementation for Layer 3:

SAP BTP - CI - Immutable Routing Principle: in SAP CPI with Value Mapping metadata ODCP in practice
Released: 07/05/2026

 

The Architecture — Immutable Facade, Metadata Resolution

External HTTP/API Consumer
(any system consuming the semantic API facade)
        │
        ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      SAP BTP API Management                          │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ LAYER 1 — GDCR                                                 │  │
│  │ Gateway Domain-Centric Routing                                 │  │
│  │                                                                │  │
│  │ Responsibility:                                                │  │
│  │   • expose one immutable semantic gateway facade per domain    │  │
│  │   • validate the semantic URL contract                         │  │
│  │   • apply authentication / access policies                     │  │
│  │   • preserve domain intent                                     │  │
│  │                                                                │  │
│  │ Example Semantic URL:                                          │  │
│  │   /sales/orders/create/salesforce                              │  │
│  └───────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                  │                                   │
│                                  ▼                                   │
│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ LAYER 2 — DDCR                                                 │  │
│  │ Domain Driven-Centric Router                                   │  │
│  │                                                                │  │
│  │ Responsibility:                                                │  │
│  │   • normalize the action                                       │  │
│  │   • build the deterministic routing key                        │  │
│  │   • resolve the backend target from KVM metadata               │  │
│  │   • fail fast when no governed route exists                    │  │
│  │   • set the resolved target.url                                │  │
│  └───────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                  │                                   │
└──────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────┘
                                   │
                                   ▼
                    ┌──────────────────────────────┐
                    │ Metadata Control Plane       │
                    │ kvm.idinterface              │
                    │                              │
                    │ route key → target profile   │
                    └───────────────┬──────────────┘
                                    │
        ┌───────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┐
        ▼                           ▼                           ▼
┌───────────────────┐     ┌────────────────────┐      ┌────────────────────┐
│ SAP CPI iFlow     │     │ Registered HTTP    │      │ SOAP/CXF-backed    │
│ SOAP/HTTP         │     │ backend            │      │ integration target │
│ ODCP governed     │     │                    │      │ via CPI mediation  │
└───────────────────┘     └────────────────────┘      └────────────────────┘

 

GDCR is the entry point to the Domain Layer. It is the contract between the outside world and the integration landscape. It never hardcodes a backend target.

DDCR is the deterministic runtime resolver. It receives the semantic request, resolves the target through metadata, and binds the request to the registered backend profile.

The gateway facade remains stable. The metadata evolves.

The KVM — Where Runtime Variability Lives

The KVM — Key Value Map — is the metadata control plane used by DDCR behind the GDCR facade.

It is the only thing that grows when a new route is added.

Each domain package has its own kvm.idinterface. In this demo, keys are domain-local. That means the route key does not need to repeat the domain token because the metadata container itself is already domain-scoped.

Demo key format:

dcrp{entity}{actioncode}{target}idNN:{protocol}

Semantic meaning:

entity      = business object
actioncode  = normalized DDCR action code
target      = vendor/system identifier
idNN        = route identifier
protocol    = registered target profile, for example http or cxf

Example:

dcrporderscsalesforceid01:http

Means:

orders  → entity
c       → create
salesforce → target
id01    → route id
http    → target profile

If an enterprise chooses to use a shared KVM across domains, the key should include the domain or use a full deterministic key. For this demo, the KVM is domain-scoped, which keeps the key compact.

SAP APIM — 4 Domain Proxies:

Domain-scoped KVMs:

SALES — kvm.idinterface

Package: cpipackage-nx.sales.o2c.integrations

Key Target
dcrporderscsalesforceid01:httphttps://<apim>/sales/orders/create/salesforce
dcrpordersusalesforceemeaid02:httphttps://<apim>/sales/orders/update/salesforceemea
dcrpcustomerssshopifyid03:httphttps://<apim>/sales/customers/sync/shopify
dcrppaymentsnstripeid04:httphttps://<apim>/sales/payments/notify/stripe
dcrporderscmicrosoftid05:cxfhttps://<apim>/sales/orders/create/microsoft
dcrpdeliveriestfedexid06:httphttps://<apim>/sales/deliveries/transfer/fedex
dcrpcustomersss4hanaid07:cxfhttps://<apim>/sales/customers/sync/s4hana
dcrppaymentsns4hanaid08:cxfhttps://<apim>/sales/payments/notify/s4hana
dcrpinvoicescquickbooksid09:cxfhttps://<apim>/sales/invoices/create/quickbooks
dcrpinvoicescs4hanaid10:cxfhttps://<apim>/sales/invoices/create/s4hana
dcrpdeliveriests4hanaid11:cxfhttps://<apim>/sales/deliveries/transfer/s4hana
dcrpreturnscshopifyid12:httphttps://<apim>/sales/returns/create/shopify

FINANCE — kvm.idinterface

Package: cpipackage-nx.finance.r2r.integrations

Key Target
dcrpinvoicescquickbooksid01:cxfhttps://<apim>/finance/invoices/create/quickbooks
dcrpinvoicescs4hanaid02:cxfhttps://<apim>/finance/invoices/create/s4hana
dcrppaymentsnstripeid03:httphttps://<apim>/finance/payments/notify/stripe
dcrppaymentsns4hanaid04:httphttps://<apim>/finance/payments/notify/s4hana
dcrpaccountssxeroid05:httphttps://<apim>/finance/accounts/sync/xero
dcrpjournalscsapid06:cxfhttps://<apim>/finance/journals/create/sap
dcrpexpensesccoupaid07:httphttps://<apim>/finance/expenses/create/coupa
dcrpreceiptsuconcurid08:httphttps://<apim>/finance/receipts/update/concur
dcrpbudgetssworkdayid09:cxfhttps://<apim>/finance/budgets/sync/workday
dcrptaxescavalaraid10:httphttps://<apim>/finance/taxes/create/avalara

LOGISTICS — kvm.idinterface

Package: cpipackage-nx.logistics.le.integrations

Key Target
dcrpshipmentscfedexid01:httphttps://<apim>/logistics/shipments/create/fedex
dcrptrackingsuupsid02:httphttps://<apim>/logistics/trackings/update/ups
dcrpdeliveriescdhlid03:cxfhttps://<apim>/logistics/deliveries/create/dhl
dcrpshipmentsqfedexid04:httphttps://<apim>/logistics/shipments/query/fedex
dcrpcontainerssmaerskid05:httphttps://<apim>/logistics/containers/sync/maersk
dcrpwarehousesuwmsid06:cxfhttps://<apim>/logistics/warehouses/update/wms
dcrpfreightsccoyoteid07:httphttps://<apim>/logistics/freights/create/coyote
dcrproutessproject44id08:httphttps://<apim>/logistics/routes/sync/project44
dcrpmanifestsccustomsid09:cxfhttps://<apim>/logistics/manifests/create/customs
dcrpinventoryswmsid10:cxfhttps://<apim>/logistics/inventory/sync/wms

PROCUREMENT — kvm.idinterface

Package: cpipackage-nx.procurement.p2p.integrations

Key Target
dcrprequisitionscaribaid01:cxfhttps://<apim>/procurement/requisitions/create/ariba
dcrpposccoupaid02:httphttps://<apim>/procurement/pos/create/coupa
dcrprfqscaribaid03:cxfhttps://<apim>/procurement/rfqs/create/ariba
dcrpcontractssjaggaerid04:httphttps://<apim>/procurement/contracts/sync/jaggaer
dcrpinvoicesabaswareid05:cxfhttps://<apim>/procurement/invoices/approve/basware
dcrpsupplierssivaluaid06:httphttps://<apim>/procurement/suppliers/sync/ivalua
dcrpcatalogsutradeshiftid07:httphttps://<apim>/procurement/catalogs/update/tradeshift
dcrpgrnscwmsid08:cxfhttps://<apim>/procurement/grns/create/wms
dcrpbuyerssoracleid09:httphttps://<apim>/procurement/buyers/sync/oracle
dcrpsourcingsqaribaid10:cxfhttps://<apim>/procurement/sourcing/query/ariba

URL Structure — APIM to CPI

The URL structure follows a deterministic semantic pattern. Once the convention is understood, every endpoint becomes self-documenting.

External APIM proxy URL:

https://{tenant}.hana.ondemand.com/{domain}/{entity}/{action}/{target}

Example — Sales Order from Salesforce entering the GDCR facade:

POST https://<apim>/sales/orders/create/salesforce

The request expresses intent:

domain  = sales
entity  = orders
action  = create
target  = salesforce

DDCR normalizes the action, builds the deterministic route key, resolves the target profile from kvm.idinterface, and sets the backend target dynamically.

The consumer sees:

/sales/orders/create/salesforce

The backend topology stays hidden.

CPI iFlow Address — ODCP Layer

When the target is SAP Cloud Integration, the target iFlow follows ODCP naming:

id{nn}.{subdomain}.{vendor}.{resource}.{system}.{operation}.{direction}.{mode}

Examples:

id01.o2c.salesforce.order.sap.c.in.sync
id07.o2c.s4hana.customer.sap.s.in.sync
id03.r2r.stripe.payment.s4hana.n.in.sync
id09.p2p.ariba.rfq.s4hana.c.in.sync

The protocol suffix in the KVM — for example :http or :cxf — tells DDCR which registered target profile to select.

Payload transformation remains the responsibility of the target integration flow.

GDCR — Responsibility and Boundaries

GDCR is the immutable semantic gateway to the Domain Layer.

What GDCR does

  • exposes a stable semantic facade;
  • receives inbound calls through a domain-level gateway contract;
  • applies authentication and API policies;
  • validates the semantic URL shape;
  • preserves domain intent;
  • hands the request to DDCR for runtime resolution.

What GDCR does not do

  • it does not transform the payload;
  • it does not own business logic;
  • it does not hardcode backend URLs;
  • it does not change when a new backend target is onboarded.

This boundary is the core of the pattern.

GDCR is the facade. DDCR is the resolver. CPI is the execution layer.

DDCR — Responsibility and Boundaries

DDCR is the deterministic domain resolution engine.

What DDCR does

  • receives the semantic request from the GDCR facade;
  • normalizes the action;
  • builds the deterministic routing key;
  • reads the domain KVM metadata;
  • resolves the registered target profile;
  • sets the resolved target.url;
  • fails fast when no governed route exists;
  • optionally captures routing timing headers in benchmark/demo mode.

What DDCR does not do

  • it does not own business logic;
  • it does not validate business payload content;
  • it does not own retry policy;
  • it does not perform domain transformations;
  • it does not require code changes when a new target is registered.

Retry, mapping, enrichment, and business validation belong to the target integration flow in SAP Cloud Integration.

Before and After

 

Area Before SDIA After GDCR + DDCR
Proxies per domain1 per interface/vendor/backend1 per domain
New vendor onboardingNew proxy + deploymentAdd 1 KVM row
New operationNew route logic / proxy changeAdd 1 KVM row
Credential scopeMixed across domainsSegmented per domain
Naming conventionInconsistentDeterministic ODCP / SDIA naming
Routing logicHardcoded in proxyResolved from KVM
Developer required for new routeUsually yesNo routing-code developer
Redeployment required for new routeUsually yesNo proxy redeployment
Backend topology exposureVisible in URLHidden behind semantic facade

Validation — Newman Parallel Load Test

The architecture was validated using Newman with 4 parallel sessions and 2 iterations across all 42 endpoints — 336 total calls across SALES, FINANCE, LOGISTICS, and PROCUREMENT domains.

The test covered:

  • REST-style JSON payloads;
  • SOAP-style XML payloads;
  • HTTP target profiles;
  • CXF target profiles;
  • 4 domain metadata containers;
  • 42 KVM routes.

All 42 endpoints resolved correctly through KVM metadata.

Result:

42 endpoints
336 calls
0 routing failures
0 misrouted calls

The collection is available on GitHub and can be executed in any SAP BTP trial account.

Basic run:

newman run GDCR-DDCR-Phantom-v12.json -n 2

Parallel load simulation on Windows:

start "S1" cmd /k newman run GDCR-DDCR-Phantom-v12.json -n 10
start "S2" cmd /k newman run GDCR-DDCR-Phantom-v12.json -n 10
start "S3" cmd /k newman run GDCR-DDCR-Phantom-v12.json -n 10
start "S4" cmd /k newman run GDCR-DDCR-Phantom-v12.json -n 10

The Principle That Connects Everything

In SAP Cloud Integration, ODCP applies the same logic to iFlows and Value Mappings:

The iFlow routing never changes. The Value Mapping grows.

In SAP BTP API Management, GDCR and DDCR apply the same logic to proxies and KVMs:

The proxy never changes. The KVM grows.

Same principle. Different layer. One architecture: SDIA.

When the landscape adds a new vendor, a new operation, or a new SAP system, the change surface becomes metadata.

No proxy redeployment.
No routing-code change.
No topology exposure in the consumer contract.

The proxy is the contract.
The KVM is the metadata control plane.
They must never be confused.

Download and Try It

Everything demonstrated in this post is available on GitHub:

  • APIM proxy artifacts for GDCR + DDCR;
  • KVM templates for all 4 domains;
  • Newman collection with parallel load test script;
  • SAP CPI iFlow stubs for target execution;
  • runnable test examples.

GitHub:

https://github.com/rhviana/deip/tree/main/gdcr-proven/gdcr-drcp-sap-api

References

SDIA v3.0          — DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18877635 — CC BY 4.0
ODCP v3.0          — DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18876593 — CC BY 4.0
DDCR V3.0          — DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18864833 — CC BY 4.0

Site:

www.domain-intent.com

Ricardo Luz Holanda Viana
Independent Solo Researcher · Enterprise Integration Architect
SAP BTP Integration Suite Expert · SAP Press e-Bite Author — Enterprise Messaging, 2021
Creator of DEIP · SDIA · GDCR · DDCR · ODCP · EDCP · DDCP

entry.title: Re: S4/HANA Migration Project ATC Check: S/4HANA: Search...
entry.content:
Hello,

The ATC message you're seeing comes from SAP Note 2296016 ("Removal of Technical Application Analysis"). The "no functional equivalent" status means exactly what it says — TAA has been removed from S/4HANA without a direct replacement. Custom code referencing LFA1 in a TAA context can't be migrated 1:1; it needs one of three handling paths:

1. Determine what the TAA usage is actually doing.

TAA was originally a technical-analysis subsystem. If your custom code uses TAA APIs to inspect database access patterns, dependencies, or runtime behavior of LFA1-related code, ask whether that analysis is still needed in S/4. Often this code was diagnostic/reporting and is no longer used in production.

2. If the LFA1 access itself is the issue (not TAA functionality).

LFA1 still exists in S/4HANA as a compatibility view, but new code should access vendor master via the Business Partner (Geschäftspartner) concept:

  • CDS view: I_Supplier or I_BusinessPartner
  • BAPI: BAPI_BUPA_* family
  • Direct table joins to BUT000 + LFB1 + LFM1 if a CDS view doesn't cover your case

If the custom code only happens to reference LFA1 incidentally and the TAA call is the simplification target, you can keep LFA1 reads and just remove the TAA call.

3. If TAA functionality is genuinely needed.

The functional equivalent in S/4HANA depends on what you were measuring. Embedded analytics (CDS analytical views), SAP Cloud ALM for runtime/landscape analysis, or Solution Manager for landscape monitoring are common substitutes. None is a drop-in replacement.

Practical workflow that scales when you have many such findings:

  • Run SYCM (System Custom Code Migration Cockpit) first to check if the affected custom code object is still actively used. Production-runtime data tells you whether it's safe to retire before refactoring.
  • Use Custom Code Migration Cockpit (CCMC) Quick Fixes for any LFA1 references that fall under standard simplification patterns — those have automated rewrites.
  • For TAA-specific code with no replacement: document the gap, retire the code, and capture the lost functionality as a separate requirement. The decision becomes "is this still needed for the business?" rather than a technical migration question.

If you're seeing this same TAA pattern across multiple custom programs, classifying them by SYCM usage (A/B/C/D — actively used / infrequent / unused 6+ months / unused 12+ months) before deciding scope saves significant effort. Out-of-scope (no production usage in 12+ months) is the cheapest disposition.

Hope this helps.

Regards,
Youssef Boukachabine

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: MS SQL Stored Procedures with Return Codes
entry.content:
This is a SAP BTP CPI iFLow JDBC Adpater related question.

We have a Stored Procedure in MS SQL DB.

In addition to regular output parameters and result-sets, it also return a code (Return Code). This RetunrCode concept is specifically only available in MS SQL DB as far as I know.

The execution/SQL Statement to capture the return code is --> 

? = {StoredProcName} (?, ?, ?, ......);

In the above SQL Statement, the all the '?' (either input or output or in/out parameters) that are in brackets, I know how to handle as it is clearly mentioned in documentation.

However, the first '?' which is supposed to capture the Return Value, I am not sure how to handle that.

I tried the below:

  1. Add an additional output parameter as first one in the Request XML. Did not work, threw error saying number of parameters did not match.
  2. Just sent as many parameters as there are in the brackets with respective fields details. Did not work, threw error saying parameter '0' details/value not set.

I need the return value to be captured in the JDBC Adapter response as the Business logic in further process depends on it. If the value is 1, need to raise an exception and send email to business.

Appreciate if anyone can help how to use JDBC Adapter to invoke the Stored Procedure successfully and capture the Return Code.

entry.title: Re: Business Objects Enterprise 4.3 update from SP04 patc...
entry.content:
Hello Andrea,

Looking at the errors and warnigns,  There is a chance that the addnode.bat and/or deployfiles.bat failed.
Check 1 -  Check the <install>setup/packagestmp folder and if you see 100s or 1000s of *dfo.xml  files, then these could not get pushed or deployed into CMS DB due to possibly temporary CMS not kicking off or the .bat scripts could not login to temporary cms
-- This can be remedied with a repair using best practices  https://me.sap.com/notes/2468513
-- However, If you have no more than 5-10 logs, then your upgrade may have succedeed and these errors in the log are probably from the cmsup checking script which can be ignored.

Another option - self log analysis - try uploading the install logs from latest timestamp folder into this link
Support log assistant(self-service link)
- if it detects an issue - it will recommend the solution - possibly repair which is the most common in these type of issues

If you want further confirmation, you can attach a zipped copy of your setupengine.log in the <install_loc>installdata/logs folder and we can try to confirm the same for you.

regards. Shiva

entry.title: Re: Deleting a modifiable transport
entry.content:
Deleting the transport will only exclude those changes to be moved to the next environments. They wont be removed/ reversed.

Good practice is to revert the changes and delete the transport or move the changes to a different transport

entry.title: Re: Problem reinstalling SAP ASE
entry.content:
I've seen the Snap::Validate problem on 16.0 SP04 PL04.   My notes on this problem:
On some CPU architectures, ASE will sometimes stack trace in the SnapValidation module during boot. To fix this add the -T11889 option to the RUN file (See SYS-480806, SAP KBA 3018138 - Server fails to boot after signal 11 with Snap::Validate - SAP ASE 16.0)

entry.title: How to delete/cancel a Key User Extensibility transport from Import Collection in 2-System Landscape
entry.content:
We would like to know how to delete or cancel a transport that has already been:

1. Created in the Test system

2. Exported via "Export Software Collection"

3. Is now visible in the Production system's "Import Collection" app with status "Ready for Import" The transport is scheduled for automatic import (e.g., at 23:00). We need to understand the process to PREVENT the import and DELETE the transport from the import queue BEFORE it gets imported automatically.

 

1. Is it possible to delete a transport from the Import Collection before it gets imported?

2. If yes, which app or function should we use? - Import Collection app? - Export Software Collection app? - Another tool?

3. If deletion is not possible in the apps, do we need to create a Service Request to SAP?

4. What is the recommended process to handle incorrect transports that should NOT be imported to production?

Thank you for your guidance. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to delete/cancel a Key User Extensibility transpo...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

 

Since you have a 2-System Landscape, there is no way to remove/discard the Key User Extensibility collection from the P system. it's possible to leave the collection in P-System for now and check all the items in Q-System. When the collection is ready to be exported, you can export a new version of the collection and then do the import in P-System.

Please check the status of the items in the collection. Sometimes the Export Software Collection app will include 'Unchanged' items into the export. E.g. after an upgrade. Status 'Unchanged' means the items have not been changed, which means it's safe to import them.

If there is any 'New', 'Changed' or 'Deleted' items that you don't want to import into the P system, do the following:

1. In the next version of the software collection the items must be brought to a state that is desired in the P system. For example:

  • Deleting a 'New' or 'Changed' Item
  • Changing a 'New' or 'Changed' to the final state that is desired
  • Recreating a 'Deleted' Item if you still need it in P-System

2. Then export the next version of the collection. In P system the import of both version should be done together. This will make sure that the final state of the items is directly imported.

 

For your reference, check KBA 3103104 - What to do if an unwanted Key User Extensibility Software Collection is exported to P-System? (2-System Landscape) - SAP S/4HANA Cloud

 

Kind regards.

entry.title: Using SAP DRC with residual payments
entry.content:

We would like to implement SAP DRC for our Mexico business unit.  However, today we use residual payment methodology in SAP, not partial payments.  We have been told that you must use partial payments in order to be able to use SAP DRC.  This seems strange to us, and residual payments are standard SAP functionality. entry.title: Re: Is it possible to assign In-App Custom App to Busines...
entry.content:
Dear @ayechan,

In Public Cloud we cannot assign an app created with Key User Extensibility to a custom Business Catalog created in Eclipse / ADT via Developer Extensibility. SAP treats these as two separate extensibility models. For Key User Extensibility apps the supported authorization path is Custom Catalog Extensions and this app supports assignment only to relevant SAP-delivered business catalogs. SAP explicitly confirms that business catalogs created via Developer Extensibility are not available in Custom Catalog Extensions and that this is expected behawior

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3484254

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3491119

SAP also confirms the same point from the Developer Extensibility side a custom business catalog created in the ABAP Environment is relevant when we create a custom application in Developer Extensibility. It is not supported to use such custom business catalogs in the Custom Catalog Extensions app for Key User Extensibility applications

Recommended workaround

The practical workaround is to keep the app in the Key User Extensibility model and assign it to the most appropriate SAP-delivered business catalog using Custom Catalog Extensions. Then we control access through a dedicated business role and launchpad page/space assignment:

  1. In the Custom Catalog Extensions app, create a Custom Catalog Extension that adds the custom app/tile to a suitable SAP-delivered business catalog and publish the extension
  2. Create or adjust a dedicated business role for the intended users
  3. Assign the required business catalog to that role
  4. Add the custom app to a launchpad page and assign the page to the launchpad space mapped to the same business role
  5. Assign only that business role to the users who should access the app

SAP Learning confirms that users can access a custom launchpad app only if they have the required business catalog assigned to their business role and that the app’s location on the launchpad is controlled separately through the launchpad page and space assigned to the same role

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/using-key-user-in-app-extensibility-to-create-a-custom-user-interface

If the concern is that the selected SAP standard business catalog contains more applications than required you should also check the IAM Apps tab in Maintain Business Roles. Since Public Cloud 2502, SAP provides app authorization variants, allowing activation or deactivation of individual supported IAM apps in a business role. This can reduce the need for a fully separate custom catalog in some scenarios

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3586595

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2633830

However this does not change the main limitation i.e still cannot move the Key User app into an ADT-created custom business catalog. It is only an access-control workaround at business-role level

Alternative option

If the requirement is strict and the app must be assigned to a fully custom business catalog then the app should be rebuilt as a Developer Extensibility custom application. In that case, the custom business catalog created in ADT can be used for the developer-created application

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Problem connecting with ODBC
entry.content:
Hi, ALL,

Now I'm trying to run my software and connect with the native ODBC driver.

 

[code]

IgorReinCloud /home/igor # cat /etc/unixODBC/odbcinst.ini
[ODBC]
Trace=yes
TraceFile=/tmp/sql.log

[Sybase]

entry.title: PowerDesigner Hangs During Generate Physical Data Model When Filter Is Applied
entry.content:
I have found that if a filter search term is entered into the Object Browser such that the target physical model is hidden and I attempt to Generate Physical Data Model from my logical data model, SAP PowerDesigner will hang indefinitely. My only option at that point is to go to Windows Task Manager and End Task on PowerDesigner

I'm working with a logical and physical data model in PowerDesigner 16.7.6.0 (7187), though I have found this to happen with previous versions as well. It would be very nice if PowerDesigner either didn't require the target physical model to be visible in the Object Browser or simply fail with an error message. As it is, I try really hard to be careful not to attempt Generate Physical Data Model with search criteria entered in the Object Browser, but every now and then, I slip.

Does anyone else experience this behavior?

entry.title: How to Create Multiple intervals in the multi shift depreciation
entry.content:
How to Create Multiple intervals for each month based on Multiple asset codes(Approximately 7000 Asset codes) & their useful life in the multi shift depreciation of fixed asset module.

Tile - Manage fixed asset

Manage Fixed Asset > Valuation >0L > Book Depreciation > General Parameters >

Intervals /Shift factor / Variable Depreciation Portion to be created for each month based on Multiple asset codes(Approximately 7000 Asset codes) & their useful life

entry.title: Inbound Email not reaching SOIN SAP Inbox in Offline Approval Workflow (Part 3 of Blog)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am implementing an offline email approval workflow based on the blog series: Offline/External Mail Approval Process

I have completed the outbound setup, but I am stuck on Part 3: Inbound Processing.

The Issue: When I send an approval email from Outlook, the message does not appear in transaction SOIN. Since it’s not reaching the inbound monitor, it never hits the business logic or the SAP Inbox.

Current Configuration & Verification:

  • SMICM: The SMTP service is showing as Active (Green) on the designated port (e.g., 25 or 25000).

  • SICF: The SAPconnect service is active, and the logon data is maintained with a system user.

  • SCOT: The default domain is maintained and matches the recipient domain.

  • Port Check: I have verified with the network team that the port is open, but still, nothing is hitting the SAP system.

It feels like a handshake issue between the external mail server and the SAP ICM. Has anyone faced a situation where the SMTP service is active in SMICM but no entries appear in SOIN? Are there specific logs in SMICM or OS-level checks I should perform to see why the connection is being dropped?
Is there a specific way we need to send the email like @ecc.dev.<companyportal>.com so that it reached sap inbox of the internal user

Thanks and regards,

Judeson D'souza

entry.title: pedido permanece en estado bloqueado (indicador “B”) a pesar de que el workflow
entry.content: In SAP MM (Materials Management), the standard purchase order release process prevents final release (transition from “Blocked” (B) to “Released” (G)) if there are inconsistencies in price determination that prevent the correct calculation of the net value of the purchase order. This behavior is not an error or an arbitrary restriction, but rather a standard SAP control mechanism designed to ensure the financial integrity of the document. As a result, SAP automatically recalculates the release status (field EKKO-FRGKE) each time a purchase order is saved, and if it detects that the net value is inconsistent or cannot be properly determined, it forces the release indicator to remain in “B” (blocked). This dynamic re‑evaluation occurs through internal routines (such as the standard functions ME_PRICING and ME_REL_STRATEGIE_EKKO during the save process), and prevents the release status from being manually manipulated (even via debugging), since any manual change will not persist: the system will overwrite the indicator according to the actual document conditions at the time of saving.

entry.title: Re: Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content:
It is very interesting and eye-opening to read that SAP ECP platform is still on SAP Netweaver 7.40 and MaxDB database. 

While SAP has been pushing the on-Prem SAP ECC customers to migrate to S4 HANA (and SF/ECP for HR Customers) for several years, it is odd that SAP has been selling customers to migrate to ECP on SAP Netweaver 7.4 and MaxDP (until a few years; all recent and new customers are on SAP HCM for S4 HANA in HDB). Many customers who migrated from their on-prem SAP ECC to SF EC and ECP thinking they were migrating to the latest and greatest weren't apparently getting them.

It is very ironical to note that while SAP was urging on-prem ECC customers to modernize to ECP, while ECP platform itself needed its own modernization.

entry.title: Re: Deprecated Classic History
entry.content:
Dear kevinarciniega,

Hope you are well. Thank you for your message.

We appreciate your concerns regarding the transition from classic stories to ODE stories in SAP Analytics Cloud. For the most up-to-date details on the deprecation timeline, please visit the blog entry titled "Important Notice: Changes in SAP Analytics Cloud C... - SAP Community" on the SAP Community site.

We understand that the optimized story experience may present some limitations. You can find specific information about these restrictions in the help document titled "About Optimized Story Experience Restrictions | SAP Help Portal" on the SAP Help Portal.

We recognize that some features from the classic story may not yet be available in the optimized version. We value your feedback and would be grateful if you could share any insights regarding the functionality differences between the two versions. Please rest assured that our development team is attentively exploring the best ways to address these gaps and to facilitate as smooth a transition as possible for all users.

Thank you for your patience and understanding as we strive to enhance your experience.

Best wishes,

Lena
